item.title: US trade court weighs legality of Trump 10% global tariff
item.description: A U.S. trade court on Friday will consider the legality of a 10% global import tax imposed by the Trump administration, which ​several states and small businesses say sidesteps a U.S. Supreme Court ruling ‌that invalidated most of Trump's previous tariffs.

item.title: US utility stocks are off to their best start since 2019. Can they keep it up?
item.description: U.S. utility stocks marked their ​strongest start to the year since 2019, benefiting from an investor retreat from riskier assets during the Iran ‌war and strong electricity demand from firms building out artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: Shippers weigh unusual routes as high air cargo rates, ocean gridlock persist
item.description: Some shippers caught between high jet fuel prices and congestion in vital Middle East waterways are now looking for transport routes ‌in unexpected places, with a recent ceasefire between Iran and the United States unlikely to offer quick relief.

item.title: Crypto giant Kraken's Fed payment account sparks concerns about risks
item.description: Crypto giant Kraken's landmark Federal Reserve master account comes with restrictions aimed at mitigating risks, but it - and others likely to follow in its wake - ​could still create vulnerabilities for the U.S. financial system.

item.title: Insight: How the AI boom derailed clean‑air efforts in one of America's most polluted cities
item.description: Barbara Johnson has been fighting coal pollution for decades in her mostly Black neighborhood of North St. Louis as an organizer with Metropolitan Congregations United – one of many activist groups campaigning for cleaner air in a city that has some of the country’s dirtiest.

item.title: US stock futures subdued ahead of inflation data, Mideast in focus
item.description: U.S. stock index futures were ​largely muted on Friday as investors stuck to the sidelines ahead of a crucial ‌inflation print, while assessing the fragility of the Middle East truce.

item.title: EU and US near critical minerals deal to combat Chinese control, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: The European Union and Washington are ​closing in on an agreement to coordinate ‌on producing and securing critical minerals, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

item.title: Apple leads global smartphone shipments in first quarter, Counterpoint says
item.description: iPhone-maker Apple ​led smartphone ‌shipments in the ​first quarter, ​growing 5% year-on-year, ⁠even ​as overall ​global shipments remained under ​pressure ​due to a ‌shortage ⁠of memory components and weak ​consumer ​sentiment, ⁠Counterpoint Research ​said ​on ⁠Friday.

item.title: Polls in Hungary and Peru next in key election year for world markets
item.description: Voters in Hungary could deliver a boon to bond investors, while Peru's weekend polls will help determine the next step for its economy, the latest in a string of key elections worldwide this year.

item.title: Daimler Truck Q1 unit sales decline on weak North America demand
item.description: Daimler ‌Truck , one of the world's largest truckmakers, on Friday ​reported a 9% ​decline in first-quarter group unit sales, weighed ⁠down by a ​sharp slowdown in North America.

item.title: SpaceX begins installing equipment at Texas facility, eyes year-end production, sources say
item.description: SpaceX has begun installing equipment at its advanced chip packaging facility in Bastrop, Texas, as the satellite and rocket ​company aims to begin production there by the end ​of this year, two sources familiar with the matter ⁠told Reuters.

item.title: Canada's Cohere, Germany's Aleph Alpha in merger talks, Handelsblatt reports
item.description: Artificial intelligence companies Cohere of Canada and Aleph ​Alpha of Germany are in talks to ‌merge and have Berlin's support for a potential deal, newspaper Handelsblatt reported late on Thursday.

item.title: Hyundai to recall over 294,000 US vehicles over seat belt anchor defect
item.description: Hyundai Motor ​America is recalling 294,128 vehicles ‌in the U.S. due to a defect that could ​cause seat belt ​anchors to detach, the U.S. ⁠National Highway Traffic Safety ​Administration said on Friday.

item.title: Porsche deliveries tumble further in first quarter
item.description: Porsche AG's deliveries slumped further in the first three months of 2026, with sharp ​declines in key markets China and the ‌United States, as loses its shine across.

item.title: Stefano Gabbana has stepped down as Dolce & Gabbana chairman, filing shows
item.description: Stefano Gabbana, co-founder ​of Italian luxury ‌fashion house Dolce & Gabbana, stepped down ​as chairman ​last January, according to ⁠a company ​filing with the ​local chamber of commerce.

item.title: TSMC's Q1 revenue jumps 35% y/y, beats market forecasts
item.description: The world's largest contract chipmaker, TSMC, reported first-quarter ​revenue of T$1.134 trillion ($35.71 billion) on Friday, rising ‌35% on the year to beat market forecasts on surging interest in artificial intelligence (AI) applications.

item.title: Sodexo cuts 2026 targets as new CEO gears to tackle long-standing issues
item.description: French food caterer Sodexo slashed its annual sales and profitability targets on Friday, citing execution challenges and its management's review of contracts and assets, which sent its shares falling 13%.

item.title: Morning Bid: 'That is not the agreement we have!'
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Gregor Stuart Hunter

item.title: US consumer inflation expected to have surged in March amid Iran war
item.description: U.S. consumer prices likely recorded their biggest increase in nearly four years in March as the war with Iran boosted oil prices and the pass-through from tariffs persisted, which would further diminish hopes for an interest rate cut this ​year.

item.title: Jury orders Abbott to pay $53 million in preterm infant formula trial, media report says
item.description: A jury in Chicago on Thursday said Abbott Laboratories must pay $53 million in compensatory damages to a group of families that had accused the company of failing to warn that its formula for premature infants can cause a potentially deadly bowel disease, according to the Chicago Tribune.

item.title: China's factories jolt back to inflation on Iran war price shock
item.description: China's factory-gate prices rose for ​the first time in 3-1/2 years in March, official data showed, in an ‌early sign that the Middle East conflict is feeding cost pressures into the world’s second-largest economy.

item.title: Japan rules out major domestic risks from private credit for now
item.description: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said on Friday private credit does not ​pose a major issue domestically at present but noted ‌risks linked to the $2 trillion industry could be discussed at next week's G7 finance meeting.

item.title: Chinese startup ShengShu raises $293 million to advance artificial general intelligence
item.description: Chinese artificial intelligence startup ShengShu Technology has raised 2 billion yuan ($292.59 million) in a funding round led by Alibaba Cloud, ​the company said on Friday, as competition intensifies in ‌China's AI sector.

item.title: Dollar set for weekly drop ahead of US-Iran peace talks
item.description: The dollar on Friday was heading for its largest weekly drop since January as other currencies gained on optimism that a ceasefire in the Gulf will hold and oil shipping will resume.

item.title: Stocks shaky as Israeli attacks on Lebanon tests Iran ceasefire
item.description: Asian stocks ticked up early on Friday but gains ​were capped as traders questioned the durability of this week's U.S.-Iran ceasefire and remained wary of fragile hopes for ‌Israel-Lebanon peace talks.

item.title: Oil prices rise after strikes on Saudi oil facilities
item.description: Oil prices rose in early trading on Friday following attacks on Saudi energy infrastructure, and as markets evaluated the risk premium from the ongoing closure of ​the Strait of Hormuz, despite a fragile truce agreed between the U.S. and ‌Iran.

item.title: SpaceX posted nearly $5 billion loss in 2025, The Information reports
item.description: Elon Musk's IPO-bound SpaceX posted a loss ​of nearly $5 billion in 2025 ‌on revenue of more than $18.5 billion, The Information reported on Thursday, citing sources.

item.title: Bessent, Powell warn bank CEOs about Anthropic model risks, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott ​Bessent and Federal ‌Reserve Chair Jerome Powell convened an urgent ​meeting with ​bank executives on Tuesday to warn ⁠them about the ​cyber risks raised ​by Anthropic's latest AI model, Bloomberg News reported, ​citing sources.

item.title: Apple to shutter its first unionized US store in Maryland
item.description: Apple said on Thursday that it will shut down its retail store in Towson, Maryland, ​the first of its U.S. locations where retail ‌employees successfully unionized in 2022.

item.title: Indonesia sends letter of reprimand to YouTube over breach of social media curbs, minister says
item.description: Indonesia has sent Google a letter of reprimand because its YouTube platform has not ​complied with new social media curbs for children, ‌a senior minister said, the first such sanction since the rules took effect last month.
